refined_prompt: Design a comprehensive and actionable lead nurturing automation system for a fence installation company. The system must address the current challenge of 20 out of 30 monthly quote requests going cold by implementing a structured follow-up process, seasonal re-engagement for older leads, and immediate escalation of hot lead signals. The output should include the strategic flow, specific message sequences for email and SMS, and recommendations for affordable automation tools.
final_output:
# Lead Nurturing Automation System for Your Fence Installation Company

**Executive Summary:** You're leaving $80,000–$120,000/month on the table by letting 20 leads go cold. This system implements a 30-day automated follow-up sequence across SMS and email, branching based on whether the lead opened their quote, with real-time alerts when leads show buying signals and seasonal campaigns to revive old leads. Implement it on GoHighLevel ($97/month), and target 4–6 additional closes per month — $16,000–$36,000 in recovered revenue.

---

## The System at a Glance

```
QUOTE SENT (Day 0)
    │
    ├── Trackable quote link generated (not a PDF attachment)
    ├── Immediate SMS confirmation (within 5 min)
    └── 24-hour behavioral timer starts
            │
            ▼
    ┌─── DID THEY OPEN THE QUOTE? ───┐
    │                                  │
   YES                                NO
(Engaged Track)                  (Cold Track)
    │                                  │
Day 1: SMS check-in              Day 1: SMS re-deliver link
Day 3: Email objection-buster    Day 3: Email value education
Day 7: Email social proof        Day 7: SMS simplified pitch
Day 14: SMS urgency/scarcity     Day 14: Email incentive offer
Day 30: Email final offer        Day 30: Email graceful close
    │                                  │
    └──────────┬───────────────────────┘
               │
    HOT SIGNAL MONITOR (runs continuously)
    → Quote revisited, link clicked, any reply = INSTANT alert to you
               │
    SEASONAL RE-ENGAGEMENT POOL (post-Day 30)
    → March, May, September campaigns
```

---

## Tool: GoHighLevel — $97/month

This is your platform. Not a combination of tools. One system.

**Why GoHighLevel specifically:**
- Built-in CRM + email + two-way SMS + pipeline view — all native
- Visual workflow builder handles every conditional branch below without code
- Website tracking pixel detects when leads revisit your quote page
- Built for local service businesses (plumbers, roofers, fencers — this is its sweet spot)
- Includes review request automation for post-install (future use)

**Setup note:** Deliver quotes via GoHighLevel's trackable link system, not as PDF email attachments. This is non-negotiable — the entire behavioral branching depends on knowing whether someone opened their quote.

---

## THE COMPLETE MESSAGE SEQUENCES

### Day 0 — Immediate (Within 5 Minutes of Sending Quote)

**Channel: SMS**

> Hi [First Name], it's [Your Name] from [Company Name]. Your fence quote just hit your inbox — take a look when you get a chance. If you have ANY questions, just reply here. I'm around all day. —[Your Name]

**Why this exists:** This is the single highest-leverage message in the system. It confirms delivery (emails get spam-filtered), creates a reply channel, and catches them while attention is fresh. Do not skip this.

**CRM action:** Tag lead as `Quote Sent | [Date] | Track: Pending`. Start 24-hour timer.

---

### ENGAGED TRACK — Lead Opened the Quote Within 24 Hours

These people are interested. They're comparing you to competitors and running the decision by their spouse. Your job: answer their unspoken objections, build trust, and compress the timeline.

---

**Day 1 — SMS**

> Hey [First Name], saw you had a chance to check out your fence quote. Any questions at all — even small ones? I'm here if you want to talk through anything. —[Your Name] [Phone Number]

**Why "even small ones":** People hesitate to reach out because they think their question is dumb. This phrase lowers the barrier. You'll get replies like "actually, yeah — does the price include the gate?" Those replies are gold.

---

**Day 3 — Email**

**Subject line:** The 3 questions every homeowner asks before picking a fence company

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> After installing fences in [City/Area] for [X] years, I've noticed every homeowner has the same three concerns before signing. Figured I'd answer them upfront:
>
> **1. "Is this price actually fair?"**
> Your quote breaks down to [$/linear foot] for [fence type]. That includes [materials], professional installation, and cleanup. We don't pad quotes with hidden fees — what you see is what you pay.
>
> **2. "How long will this fence actually last?"**
> The [material type] we quoted comes with a [X]-year manufacturer warranty. With our installation standards, most customers get [X+] years before any maintenance is needed. [Include one specific detail about your installation method — e.g., "We set posts in concrete 36 inches deep, not the 24-inch minimum most companies use."]
>
> **3. "What about permits and HOA approval?"**
> We handle everything — building permits, 811 utility locates, and HOA submissions. You don't make a single phone call.
>
> [BEFORE/AFTER PHOTO from a local project]
>
> Hit reply with your biggest question — I answer every email personally.
>
> —[Your Name]
> [Company Name]
> [Phone Number]

---

**Day 7 — Email**

**Subject line:** [Neighborhood name] just got their new fence — here's how it turned out

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> Wanted to share a project we just wrapped up in [neighborhood/area near the lead]:
>
> [PHOTO of completed fence]
>
> **The project:** [X] linear feet of [fence type] for [brief homeowner context — "a family with two dogs who needed a secure backyard" / "a homeowner who wanted privacy from a busy street"]
>
> **Timeline:** Installed in [X] days from signing
>
> **What they said:** "[Actual customer quote from a Google review — e.g., 'They showed up on time, finished a day early, and the fence looks incredible. Wish we'd done this years ago.']" — [First name, neighborhood] ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
>
> Want to see more projects like yours? [TRACKABLE LINK to portfolio page]
>
> Your quote is still locked in at the price we discussed. Happy to answer any questions.
>
> —[Your Name]

**CRM rule:** If lead clicks the portfolio link 2+ times → trigger Hot Signal alert immediately.

---

**Day 14 — SMS**

> Hi [First Name], quick heads-up — our crew schedule for [current month or next month] is filling up fast. If you want to lock in your quote price and grab a spot on the calendar, just reply YES and I'll hold it for you. No pressure either way. —[Your Name]

**Why this works:** Crew capacity is genuinely limited for fence installers. This isn't fake scarcity — it's real. The single-word reply "YES" reduces friction to almost nothing.

---

**Day 30 — Email**

**Subject line:** Your fence quote expires in 7 days — one last thing

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> Your quote for the [fence type] project has been sitting for about a month, and I want to give you a heads-up — material pricing is only guaranteed through [specific date, 7 days out].
>
> Before it expires, I'd like to offer one thing: **[Choose ONE that protects your margins]**
> - A free gate upgrade (standard to premium) — value of $[X]
> - OR: Permit fees waived — saves you $[X]
> - OR: 5% off your total — $[X] savings
>
> **To claim it:** Reply to this email or call [number] by [date].
>
> If now's not the right time, I completely understand. Just reply "later" and I'll make a note to check back when fence season picks up again. No hard feelings.
>
> —[Your Name]

**CRM action on "later" reply:** Move to Seasonal Re-engagement Pool. Tag `Self-ID: Timing Issue | Priority`.

---

### COLD TRACK — Lead Did NOT Open the Quote Within 24 Hours

These leads either missed your email, got distracted, or submitted requests to five companies. Strategy: re-deliver, then sell the *concept* of a fence (not your quote) to reignite interest.

---

**Day 1 — SMS**

> Hey [First Name], [Your Name] from [Company Name] here. Wanted to make sure your fence quote came through — email can be finicky. Here's a direct link just in case: [QUOTE URL]. Let me know if you have questions!

**CRM rule:** If they click this link and open the quote, wait 24 hours and re-evaluate. If opened → switch to Engaged Track at Day 3 (skip Engaged Day 1 since you just texted them).

---

**Day 3 — Email**

**Subject line:** Is a fence actually worth it in [City]? (the math surprised me)

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> Before you decide on your fence project, thought you'd find this interesting:
>
> **The money angle:** A new fence adds 2–5% to your home's resale value in [City/region]. On a $[local median home price] home, that's $[calculated amount] in equity — often more than the fence costs.
>
> **The practical angle:** Beyond property value, homeowners tell us their top 3 reasons are:
> - Kids and pets play safely without supervision stress
> - Privacy from neighbors and street traffic
> - Clear property boundaries (prevents disputes — trust me, we've seen it)
>
> **The insurance angle:** Some homeowners insurance policies offer lower premiums with a fenced yard, especially if you have a pool or trampoline. Worth a quick call to your agent.
>
> Your personalized quote is still ready whenever you want to take a look: [QUOTE LINK]
>
> —[Your Name]

---

**Day 7 — SMS**

> Hi [First Name], quick thought — most of our [City] customers say their only regret is not getting their fence sooner. Your quote's still ready whenever you are: [QUOTE URL]. No expiration pressure, just here when you need me. —[Your Name]

---

**Day 14 — Email**

**Subject line:** Still thinking about that fence? Here's a reason to take another look.

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> I know fence projects can sit on the back burner — it's a big decision. Wanted to let you know:
>
> **We're offering [same incentive as Engaged Track Day 30 — free gate upgrade / waived permits / 5% off] for projects booked by [date 7 days out].**
>
> Here's what one recent customer said:
>
> "[Testimonial — e.g., 'We went back and forth for months. Once we finally pulled the trigger, the whole project took 3 days. Should have done it a year ago.']" — [First name], [Neighborhood]
>
> Your quote: [QUOTE LINK]
>
> Reply or call [number] if you want to chat. Zero pressure.
>
> —[Your Name]

---

**Day 30 — Email**

**Subject line:** Closing your file (unless you say otherwise)

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> I don't want to clutter your inbox, so this will be my last message for a while.
>
> If you'd like me to reach out when [spring / next fence season] arrives with a fresh quote, just reply **"spring"** and I'll make a note.
>
> Otherwise, I'll step back. Your quote link stays active if you ever want to revisit it: [QUOTE LINK]
>
> It was great connecting with you. We're here whenever the timing is right.
>
> —[Your Name]

**CRM action:** Move to Seasonal Re-engagement Pool. Tag with engagement history.

---

## HOT SIGNAL ESCALATION — The Highest-ROI Component

When a lead shows buying behavior at ANY point (during the 30-day sequence or after), automation stops and you pick up the phone.

| Signal | What It Means | Your Response |
|---|---|---|
| Quote page revisited 2+ times in 48 hours | They're comparing you to another bid | Call within 1 hour |
| Portfolio/testimonial links clicked 2+ times | They're building confidence, close to deciding | Call within 2 hours |
| Any reply to SMS or email (even "not now") | Active engagement — they're thinking about it | Personal response within 30 minutes |
| Website session >5 min on services/pricing pages | Deep research mode | Text within 2 hours |

**GoHighLevel setup:** Configure workflow triggers to send you a push notification + SMS alert with the lead's name, phone number, and which signal fired. When a hot signal fires, the lead is **automatically removed from the automation sequence** — you're in control now. Automated messages after a hot signal feel robotic and kill momentum.

**Key insight:** A reply of "not interested right now" is still a hot signal. It's active engagement. Your response: "Totally understand — mind if I ask what changed? Want to make sure I'm not missing something." Tag as `Objection: Timing` and move to Seasonal Pool with priority flag.

---

## SEASONAL RE-ENGAGEMENT CAMPAIGNS

### Campaign Windows

| Campaign | Send Dates | Hook |
|---|---|---|
| **Spring Push** | March 1–7 | "Book in March, get first pick of crew dates" |
| **Early Summer** | May 12–18 | "Get it done before July 4th cookout season" |
| **Fall** | September 1–7 | "Last window before winter — protect your yard year-round" |

### Campaign Sequence (2 touches only — respect the dormancy)

**Touch 1 — Email:**

**Subject line:** Your fence quote from [original month] — ready to revisit?

**Body:**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> We talked back in [month] about your [fence type] project at [address if you have it].
>
> [Season] is here, and our crew calendar is starting to fill. I'd love to dust off your quote with updated pricing and get you on the schedule if the timing is better now.
>
> **[If tagged "Self-ID: Timing Issue":]** You mentioned last time that the timing wasn't quite right. Is now better? I can have a refreshed quote to you by end of day.
>
> **[If tagged "Never Opened":]** In case you missed it before, here's what we'd quoted for your project: [QUOTE LINK]
>
> Reply "update" and I'll send a fresh quote within 24 hours.
>
> —[Your Name]

**Touch 2 — SMS (3 days later, only if no engagement on email):**

> Hi [First Name], [Your Name] from [Company]. [Spring/Summer/Fall] fence season is here — want me to refresh your quote from [month]? Reply YES and I'll have it to you today. —[Your Name]

**Hard rule:** If zero engagement on both touches, do NOT contact again until the next seasonal window. After 2 full seasonal cycles with zero engagement (roughly 8–12 months), archive the lead permanently.

### Seasonal Outreach Priority Order:
1. Leads who opened quote + clicked links but didn't close (highest intent)
2. Leads who replied "later" or "spring" (self-identified timing)
3. Leads who opened quote but never engaged further
4. Leads who never opened (exclude after 2 cycles of silence)

---

## IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP

### Week 1 (April 2026)
- [ ] Sign up for GoHighLevel ($97/month)
- [ ] Import your existing lead database — tag every lead with status (`Quoted | Cold | Closed`) and last interaction date
- [ ] Set up your trackable quote delivery system (GoHighLevel page or tracked link — NOT PDF attachments)
- [ ] Install GoHighLevel tracking pixel on your website
- [ ] Build and activate the Day 0 SMS trigger

### Week 2
- [ ] Build the full Engaged Track sequence (Day 1 through Day 30)
- [ ] Build the full Cold Track sequence (Day 1 through Day 30)
- [ ] Configure the behavioral split (quote opened vs. not opened at 24 hours)
- [ ] Write and load your before/after photos, testimonials, and portfolio content

### Week 3
- [ ] Configure Hot Signal triggers and push notification alerts to your phone
- [ ] Build the auto-remove rule (hot signal → exit automation)
- [ ] Test the ENTIRE system end-to-end using your own phone and email as a dummy lead
- [ ] Test both tracks — verify the behavioral branching works correctly

### Week 4
- [ ] Activate the system for all new incoming quote requests
- [ ] Build your first seasonal
strategist_output:
# Lead Nurturing Automation System for Fence Installation Company
## Unified Strategic Analysis — April 2026

---

## BASELINE & OBJECTIVE

**Current state:** 30 quote requests/month → 8–10 closes (27–33%) → ~20 leads go cold

**Revenue at stake:** At an average job value of $4,000–$6,000, those 20 cold leads represent $80,000–$120,000/month in unrealized revenue.

**Target:** Recover 4–6 additional closes/month through structured nurture, raising close rate to 40–50%. This represents $16,000–$36,000 in recovered monthly revenue with near-zero incremental ad spend.

**Critical assumption:** Leads provide both email and phone at quote request. Quote is delivered via trackable link (not static PDF attachment), enabling the behavioral branching that makes this entire system work.

---

## SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

```
QUOTE SENT (Day 0)
    │
    ├── Tracking pixel + unique quote link activated
    ├── SMS confirmation sent within 5 minutes
    └── 24-hour timer starts
            │
            ▼
    ┌─── BEHAVIORAL SPLIT (Hour 24) ───┐
    │                                    │
QUOTE OPENED                     QUOTE NOT OPENED
(Engaged Track)                  (Cold Track)
    │                                    │
Day 1: SMS — personal check-in    Day 1: SMS — re-deliver quote link
Day 3: Email — objection content  Day 3: Email — "why fence now" value hook
Day 7: Email — social proof       Day 7: SMS — simplified benefit pitch
Day 14: SMS — urgency/scarcity    Day 14: Email — last incentive offer
Day 30: Email — final offer       Day 30: Email — graceful close
    │                                    │
    └──────────┬─────────────────────────┘
               │
    HOT SIGNAL MONITOR (runs continuously)
               │
    SEASONAL RE-ENGAGEMENT POOL (post-Day 30)
```

**Key design rationale confirmed across all models:** Email carries detailed content (objection handling, case studies, portfolio). SMS carries urgency, personal touch, and re-engagement pings. The alternating pattern prevents channel fatigue while exploiting SMS's ~98% open rate for critical touchpoints.

---

## PART 1: THE 30-DAY FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCE

### Pre-Sequence Trigger (Day 0 — Immediate)

**SMS (within 5 minutes of quote email):**
> "Hi [First Name], it's [Rep] from [Company]. Your fence quote just landed in your inbox — check it out when you get a sec. Reply here with any questions, happy to help! —[Rep]"

**Why this matters:** One model uniquely identified that the Day 0 SMS bridges the gap for leads who may have spam-filtered or overlooked the email. This is the single highest-leverage message in the entire system — it confirms delivery and creates a reply channel before attention fades.

**CRM action:** Tag lead as `Quote Sent | Date | Track: Pending`. Start 24-hour behavioral timer.

---

### ENGAGED TRACK (Quote Opened Within 24 Hours)

These leads have demonstrated intent. The strategy is consultative: answer their likely objections, build trust, and compress the decision timeline.

**Day 1 — SMS (Personal Check-In)**
> "Hey [First Name], saw you had a chance to look at your fence quote. Any questions at all — even small ones? I'm here. Reply or call [number]. —[Rep]"

**Purpose:** Humanize. The "even small ones" phrasing (from one strong model output) lowers the barrier to reply — people hesitate to ask what they think are dumb questions.

**Day 3 — Email (Objection Killer)**
- **Subject line:** "The 3 questions every homeowner asks before choosing a fence company"
- **Content structure:**
  - Address the top 3 objections head-on: (1) "Is this price fair?" → material/labor breakdown transparency; (2) "How long will it last?" → warranty details + material specs; (3) "What about permits/HOA?" → "We handle everything — permits, 811 utility locates, HOA coordination."
  - Include ONE specific before/after project photo from a local neighborhood (geo-relevant if possible)
  - CTA: "Reply with your biggest concern — I'll answer personally"

**Why objections on Day 3 specifically:** By this point, engaged leads have reviewed the quote and are likely comparison shopping or discussing with a spouse/partner. Proactively answering objections before they become deal-killers is higher-leverage than generic "just checking in" messages.

**Day 7 — Email (Social Proof)**
- **Subject line:** "[Neighbor's neighborhood] just got their fence installed — here's how it went"
- **Content:**
  - Customer testimonial with specific details (fence type, yard challenge solved, timeline)
  - Star rating or Google review screenshot
  - Portfolio link (trackable — triggers hot signal if clicked)
  - If available: short 30-second video walkthrough of a completed project
- **Tracking:** Multiple clicks on portfolio link → escalate to sales immediately

**Day 14 — SMS (Urgency + Scarcity)**
> "Hi [First Name], quick heads up — our crew schedule for [month] is filling up. If you want to lock in your quote price and get on the calendar, just reply 'YES' and I'll hold your spot. —[Rep]"

**Why this works:** The scarcity is real for fence installers (crew capacity is genuinely limited), which makes this authentic rather than gimmicky. The single-word reply CTA ("YES") reduces friction to near zero.

**Day 30 — Email (Final Offer + Graceful Exit)**
- **Subject line:** "Your fence quote expires in 7 days — one last thing"
- **Content:**
  - Offer a concrete incentive: free gate upgrade, 5% discount, or waived permit fee (choose one that protects margins)
  - Set clear deadline: "This quote and offer are valid through [date]"
  - Graceful opt-down: "If now's not the right time, no worries — we'll check back in when fence season picks up. Just reply 'later' and I'll make a note."
- **CRM action on "later" reply:** Move to Seasonal Re-engagement Pool with tag `Self-identified: timing issue`

---

### COLD TRACK (Quote NOT Opened Within 24 Hours)

These leads may have missed the email, lost interest, or submitted quote requests to multiple companies. The strategy is re-engagement first, then value-based persuasion.

**Day 1 — SMS (Re-Delivery)**
> "Hey [First Name], [Rep] from [Company] here. Wanted to make sure you got your fence quote — sometimes email is tricky. Here's a direct link: [URL]. Let me know if you have any questions!"

**Purpose:** Solve the delivery problem before assuming disinterest. This single message will convert some Cold Track leads into Engaged Track leads (re-check behavior after 24 hours and re-route if quote is now opened).

**Day 3 — Email (Value Hook — Not Quote-Focused)**
- **Subject line:** "Is a fence actually worth it in [City]? (the math might surprise you)"
- **Content:** Shift away from the quote entirely. Lead with value:
  - Property value increase data (fencing adds 2–5% to home value on average)
  - Safety/privacy angle for families with kids or pets
  - Insurance/liability considerations
  - Embedded quote link as secondary CTA
- **Tone:** Educational, zero-pressure

**Day 7 — SMS (Simplified Benefit)**
> "Hi [First Name], quick thought — most of our [City] customers say their biggest regret is not getting their fence sooner. Your quote's still ready whenever you are: [URL] —[Rep]"

**Day 14 — Email (Last Incentive)**
- **Subject line:** "We're holding your quote — here's a reason to take another look"
- **Content:** Offer same incentive as Engaged Track Day 30 (free gate upgrade, discount, waived fee). Include a single strong testimonial. Clear expiration date.

**Day 30 — Email (Graceful Archive)**
- **Subject line:** "Closing your file (unless you say otherwise)"
- **Content:** Brief, respectful close. "We don't want to clutter your inbox. If you'd like us to reach out when [spring/fence season] arrives, just reply 'spring' — otherwise, we'll step back. Your quote is always here: [URL]"
- **CRM action:** Move to Seasonal Pool. Tag with engagement history.

---

## PART 2: HOT SIGNAL ESCALATION

This is the highest-ROI component of the entire system. A lead exhibiting buying signals at any point during the 30-day sequence (or after) should bypass automation and receive immediate human outreach.

### Trigger Events → Immediate Sales Notification

| Signal | Threshold | Response Protocol |
|---|---|---|
| Quote page revisited | 2+ visits within 48 hours | Sales call within 1 hour |
| Portfolio/testimonial link clicked | 2+ clicks across any emails | Sales call within 2 hours |
| Reply to any SMS or email | Any reply, even "not now" | Personal response within 30 min |
| Pricing/financing page visited | Any visit | Sales call within 1 hour |
| Website session >5 min on key pages | Single session | SMS from rep within 2 hours |

### Notification Mechanism
- CRM internal alert + push notification to rep's phone
- If using GoHighLevel or HubSpot: configure webhook → Slack/SMS alert to sales rep
- **Critical rule:** Hot signal leads are REMOVED from automation immediately. Human takes over. Automated messages following a hot signal feel tone-deaf and can kill momentum.

**Unique insight worth highlighting:** One model correctly identified that even a negative reply ("not interested right now") is a hot signal — it's active engagement that gives the sales rep a conversation to work with. Tag these as `Objection: Timing` and route to seasonal pool with priority flag.

---

## PART 3: SEASONAL RE-ENGAGEMENT

### Strategy & Timing

Fence installation demand follows a predictable seasonal curve. Re-engagement campaigns target the two highest-intent windows:

| Campaign | Send Window | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Spring Push** | Early March (March 1–7) | Homeowners planning outdoor projects; crews not yet fully booked |
| **Early Summer** | Mid-May (May 12–18) | Last chance for summer installation; "get it done before July 4th" framing |
| **Fall Maintenance** | September | Secondary season; pair with "winterize your property" messaging |

### Sequence Per Campaign (2 touches, not 5 — respect the dormancy)

**Touch 1 — Email:**
- **Subject line (Spring example):** "Your fence quote from [month] — ready to revisit?"
- **Content:**
  - Acknowledge time has passed: "We chatted back in [month] about your [fence type] project"
  - Seasonal hook: "Spring is our busiest season — homeowners who book in March get first pick of crew dates"
  - Updated offer: refresh the original quote with current pricing (materials costs may have shifted)
  - If lead was tagged `Self-identified: timing issue` — lead with: "You mentioned the timing wasn't right before. Is now better?"

**Touch 2 — SMS (3 days after email, only if no engagement):**
> "Hi [First Name], [Rep] from [Company]. Spring fence season is here — want me to dust off your quote and see if anything's changed? Reply YES and I'll send an updated version. —[Rep]"

**CRM rule:** If no engagement on either touch, do NOT send again until next seasonal window. Over-contacting dormant leads burns the list.

### Segmentation Priority
Rank seasonal outreach by original engagement level:
1. **Highest priority:** Leads who opened quote + clicked links but didn't close
2. **Medium priority:** Leads who replied "later" or self-identified timing issues
3. **Lowest priority:** Leads who never opened quote (consider excluding after 2 seasonal cycles with zero engagement)

---

## PART 4: TOOL RECOMMENDATIONS

### Primary Recommendation: **GoHighLevel** (~$97/month)

**Why GoHighLevel wins for this use case:**
- Built-in CRM + email + SMS + pipeline management in one platform
- Native two-way SMS (critical — leads reply to SMS constantly)
- Visual automation/workflow builder with conditional branching
- Website tracking pixel for hot signal detection
- Built specifically for local service businesses
- Reputation management (review requests post-installation) included

### Alternative Options:

| Tool | Monthly Cost | Best For | Limitation |
|---|---|---|---|
| **HubSpot Free + Twilio SMS** | $0 + ~$20/mo SMS | Companies wanting free CRM with growth path | SMS requires technical integration |
| **Mailchimp + SimpleTexting** | ~$30 + ~$39/mo | Email-heavy approach, simple setup | No unified pipeline; manual hot signal tracking |
| **ActiveCampaign (Lite)** | ~$29/mo | Sophisticated email automation + lead scoring | SMS requires add-on; steeper learning curve |

**Verdict:** GoHighLevel is the clear choice for a single-location fence company. It eliminates the need to duct-tape multiple tools together, and its workflow builder can implement every conditional branch described in this strategy without custom code.

---

## PART 5: IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP

### Week 1–2: Foundation
- Set up GoHighLevel (or chosen tool)
- Import existing lead database; tag by status and last interaction date
- Configure trackable quote delivery (unique link per lead)
- Build Day 0 + Day 1 touchpoints for both tracks

### Week 3: Core Automation
- Build remaining 30-day sequence (both tracks, all conditional branches)
- Configure hot signal triggers and sales notification routing
- Test full sequence with internal team members as dummy leads

### Week 4: Launch + Seasonal Setup
- Activate system for all new incoming quote requests
- Build first seasonal re-engagement campaign for existing cold leads
- Run initial seasonal campaign immediately (April is prime fence season)

### Month 2+: Optimize
- Review open rates, reply rates, and conversion at each touchpoint
- A/B test subject lines on Day 3 and Day 7 emails (these are highest-leverage)
- Track which hot signals most reliably predict conversion → weight those triggers
- Measure incremental closes against baseline 8–10/month

---

## KEY METRICS TO TRACK

| Metric | Baseline | Target (90 Days) |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly closes | 8–10 | 12–15 |
| Quote-to-close rate | 27–33% | 40–50% |
| Lead response rate (any reply) | Unknown (likely <10%) | 25–35% |
| Hot signal → close conversion | N/A | 50%+ |
| Seasonal re-engagement → close | N/A | 10–15% of pool |
| Revenue recovered from nurture | $0 | $16,000–$36,000/month |

---

## WHAT THIS STRATEGY DOES NOT COVER (AND SHOULDN'T)

- **Lead generation/acquisition** — this system assumes 30 inbound leads/month; increasing that is a separate initiative
- **Post-close referral/review automation** — high value but a distinct system (GoHighLevel supports it when ready)
- **Lead qualification/scoring at intake** — worth adding in Month 2+ to route high-value projects (e.g., commercial, 200+ linear feet) to different sequences with higher-touch outreach

label: GPT-4o (standalone)
final_output:
Designing a lead nurturing automation for your fence installation company requires a strategic approach to ensure consistent engagement with potential customers. Below is a structured plan with detailed message sequences and tool recommendations to help you achieve a higher conversion rate:

### 1. Initial Setup

**Tools Recommendation:**
- **CRM with Automation Capabilities:** Consider using tools like **HubSpot Starter** or **ActiveCampaign**, both offering automation features and within your budget.
- **Email and Text Automation:** Use **Twilio** for SMS integration and the native email capabilities of the CRM.

### 2. Lead Nurturing Sequence

#### Day 1: Immediate Follow-Up

- **Email:**
  - **Subject:** "Your Fence Installation Quote is Ready!"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    Thank you for requesting a quote for your fence installation with [Your Company Name]. We have prepared your estimate and are excited to help you enhance your property.

    [Include a link to the quote]

    If you have any questions, feel free to reply to this email or call us at [Your Phone Number].

    Best regards,
    [Your Name]
    [Your Company Name]
    ```

- **Text:**
  - "Hi [Customer Name], your fence installation quote is ready. Check your email or call us at [Your Phone Number] for more details."

#### Day 3: Reminder and Engagement

- **Email (If Quote Not Opened):**
  - **Subject:** "Have You Seen Your Quote Yet?"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    We noticed you haven’t opened your fence installation quote yet. We’re here to help with any questions you might have.

    [Resend link to the quote]

    Looking forward to assisting you,

    [Your Name]
    ```
  
- **Text:**
  - "Hi [Customer Name], we noticed you haven't checked your fence quote yet. Let us know if you need any assistance!"

#### Day 7: Value Proposition

- **Email:**
  - **Subject:** "Why [Your Company Name] for Your Fence Installation?"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    Choosing the right company for your fence installation is crucial. Here’s why [Your Company Name] stands out:

    - [Feature 1: e.g., High-quality materials]
    - [Feature 2: e.g., Experienced installation team]
    - [Feature 3: e.g., Competitive pricing]

    [Link to customer testimonials or case studies]

    We’re here to discuss how we can meet your needs.

    Best,
    [Your Name]
    ```

#### Day 14: Offer or Incentive

- **Email:**
  - **Subject:** "Special Offer Just for You!"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    As a thank you for considering us, we’re offering a [discount/bonus service] if you decide to proceed with your fence installation this month.

    [Include offer details]

    Let’s secure your spot today!

    Cheers,
    [Your Name]
    ```

#### Day 30: Final Check-in

- **Email:**
  - **Subject:** "Still Interested in Enhancing Your Property?"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    It’s been a month since you received your quote, and we wanted to check in. We’re here to answer any questions you might still have.

    Remember, our team is ready to provide the best service for your fence installation needs.

    Best wishes,
    [Your Name]
    ```

### 3. Seasonal Re-engagement for Old Leads

- **Quarterly Email:**
  - **Subject:** "Ready for a New Look This Season?"
  - **Body:**
    ```
    Hi [Customer Name],

    New season, new opportunities to upgrade your property! If you’re considering a fence installation, now’s the perfect time.

    [Highlight any seasonal promotions or new offerings]

    Let’s reconnect and discuss your needs.

    Warm regards,
    [Your Name]
    ```

### 4. Immediate Escalation for Hot Signals

**Automation Setup:**
- Use your CRM’s automation to monitor actions such as quote page visits. Set up an immediate notification (via email or text) to yourself or your sales team when such actions occur.

**Example Alert:**
- "Alert: [Customer Name] revisited their quote page. Consider reaching out for potential closing opportunity."

### Conclusion

Implementing this automated lead nurturing strategy will help maintain consistent communication with prospects, increase engagement, and ultimately improve your conversion rates. By using recommended tools within your budget, you'll streamline your follow-up process and focus on converting those leads more effectively.

label: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (standalone)
final_output:
# Lead Nurturing Automation System for Fence Installation Company

## System Architecture Overview

Before writing the sequences, here's the logic framework that governs everything:

**Three lead states you'll be managing:**
1. **Cold Lead** — Quote sent, no open detected
2. **Warm Lead** — Quote opened but no response
3. **Hot Lead** — Quote opened multiple times OR revisited the quote link (immediate escalation)

**The math on why this matters:** If you close 8-10 of 30 leads now, recovering even 15% of your 20 cold leads = 3 additional jobs per month. At an average fence job of $3,500, that's $10,500/month in recovered revenue from automation that costs you ~$80/month.

---

## Tool Stack Recommendation (Total: ~$75-85/month)

### Primary Tools

**1. GoHighLevel — $97/month (best all-in-one option)**
- Handles SMS + email automation in one platform
- Built-in quote link tracking (know when they open it)
- Pipeline view to see all 30 leads visually
- Can replace your CRM, email tool, AND text tool
- Automatically triggers "hot lead" alerts when someone revisits a page

**Alternative if GoHighLevel is over budget:**

**1. Jobber — $49/month (field service specific)**
- Quote tracking with open notifications
- Client follow-up reminders
- Integrates with your quoting workflow naturally

**2. SimpleTexting — $29/month**
- Two-way SMS automation
- Merge tags for personalized texts
- Compliance built in (TCPA)

**3. Mailchimp — Free tier**
- Email sequences with open tracking
- Automation triggers based on behavior

**Total alternative stack: ~$78/month**

**My recommendation: GoHighLevel.** For a service business doing 30 leads/month, having everything in one place prevents you from manually connecting tools and actually running the system. The $97 pays for itself with one recovered job.

---

## Quote Delivery Setup (Before the Sequences Begin)

**Critical: How you send the quote determines what you can track.**

Instead of attaching a PDF, send quotes as a **trackable link** (GoHighLevel does this natively, or use PandaDoc's free tier). This gives you:
- Open timestamp
- Number of views
- Time spent on document
- Automatic trigger to your hot lead alert

**Your quote email should include this footer:**
> *"This quote is saved at the link above and won't expire for 30 days. If your project timeline changes, just reply to this email — we hold our material pricing for return clients."*

This plants the seed that coming back later is normal and welcomed.

---

## THE MESSAGE SEQUENCES

### SEQUENCE A: They Haven't Opened the Quote
*(Triggered when: 24 hours pass with zero opens detected)*

---

**DAY 1 — Text Message (sent ~10am)**

```
Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Company]. 
I sent your fence quote yesterday — wanted to make sure 
it didn't get buried in email. Total came to $[Amount] 
for the [X linear ft / wood privacy / chain link] you 
needed. Any questions I can answer? Reply here anytime.
```

*Why this works: You're solving a real problem (email deliverability), referencing specific details from their job so it doesn't feel automated, and opening a text conversation which has 98% open rates vs. 20% for email.*

---

**DAY 3 — Email**

**Subject line:** One thing most fence quotes don't mention

```
Hi [First Name],

I realized I probably should have included this in 
your original quote, so wanted to follow up.

[CHOOSE THE ONE MOST RELEVANT TO THEIR JOB TYPE:]

FOR WOOD FENCES:
The quote I sent uses pressure-treated pine rated for 
ground contact (UC4B). A lot of quotes you'll get will 
spec cheaper wood — looks identical on paper but rots 
2-3x faster. Wanted you to have that context if you're 
comparing estimates.

FOR VINYL:
The posts in your quote are set in concrete with a 
minimum 3-foot depth. Some installers skip concrete 
on vinyl to save time. It's why you see vinyl fences 
leaning after a few years.

FOR CHAIN LINK:
Your quote includes tension wire at the bottom. 
Without it, the fabric lifts over time — especially 
if you have dogs.

If any of this raises questions — or if you want me 
to walk through the quote on a quick call — just 
reply here or call me at [Phone].

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]
```

*Why this works: This is value, not a sales pitch. You're educating them on something specific to their job type that makes them smarter buyers. This positions you as the expert, not just the cheapest option.*

---

**DAY 7 — Text Message**

```
Hey [First Name] — [Your Name] here. Checking in on 
the fence quote I sent last week. No pressure at all, 
but lumber and material costs have been jumping around 
lately and I want to make sure I can hold this price 
for you. Are you still planning the project this season?
```

*Why this works: The material cost angle is legitimate (it's true in the fence industry) and creates a soft urgency without being pushy. The question invites a response — even "not yet" gives you information.*

---

**DAY 14 — Email**

**Subject line:** Still thinking about the [Street Name / neighborhood] fence?

```
Hi [First Name],

It's been a couple weeks since I sent your quote, 
and I wanted to reach out one more time before I 
close out this estimate in my system.

A few things that might be helpful:

**If budget was a concern:**
I can look at adjusting the scope — sometimes 
switching one section to a different material, 
or phasing the project, brings the number into 
a more comfortable range without gutting the plan.

**If timing wasn't right:**
No problem at all. Just reply with "save it" and 
I'll keep your quote on file. When you're ready 
to revisit, I can re-price it based on current 
material costs (sometimes better, sometimes worse 
— I'll be straight with you).

**If you went with someone else:**
Totally understand. If you're ever unhappy with 
the work or need additional fencing down the road, 
I'd love the chance to earn your business then.

Is any of these where you're at?

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]
```

*Why this works: You're addressing their three most likely objections directly without them having to say anything awkward. The "save it" option is genius — it keeps cold leads warm without them having to commit to anything.*

---

**DAY 30 — Text Message**

```
[First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Company]. 
Last touchpoint from me on your fence quote — I don't 
want to be a bother. If the project's on hold, just 
reply LATER and I'll reach back out in the spring. 
If it's off the table entirely, reply DONE and I'll 
leave you alone. Either way, no hard feelings.
```

*Why this works: Giving people an easy opt-out actually increases responses. The LATER keyword can trigger a tag in GoHighLevel that automatically moves them to your seasonal re-engagement list. DONE removes them and keeps your list clean. Many people who were ignoring you will reply LATER — that's a warm lead you just recovered.*

---

### SEQUENCE B: They Opened the Quote But Didn't Respond
*(Triggered when: Quote opened, but no reply within 4 hours)*

---

**DAY 1 — Text Message (sent 2 hours after open detected)**

```
Hi [First Name] — saw you had a chance to look over 
the quote. Did everything make sense? Happy to answer 
any questions or walk through the line items if 
anything looks off.
```

*Why this works: Timing matters enormously here. Two hours after they opened it, it's still fresh in their mind. The message is casual and helpful, not "I SEE YOU OPENED IT, BUY NOW."*

---

**DAY 3 — Email**

**Subject line:** Quick question about your fence quote

```
Hi [First Name],

Thanks for taking a look at the estimate. I wanted 
to follow up because sometimes people have questions 
they don't want to seem "dumb" asking — but there 
are no dumb questions in this process.

Here are the ones I get most:

**"Can the start date move if I'm not ready?"**
Yes. Once you approve the quote, we schedule 
installation based on your availability, not ours. 
Most jobs start within 10-14 days of approval.

**"What if the price changes before we start?"**
Your approved quote locks in the price. The only 
exception is if you change the scope after approval.

**"What happens if weather delays installation?"**
We reschedule at no cost to you. Fence posts need 
the ground to be workable, and we won't rush that.

**"Do I need to do anything before you arrive?"**
We'll need to call 811 to mark utilities (we 
handle this). If you have a HOA, approval before 
installation is on you, but we can give you the 
spec sheet they usually ask for.

Still have a different question? Just reply here.

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]
```

---

**DAY 7 — Text Message**

```
Hey [First Name], [Your Name] from [Company]. 
Quick check-in on the fence. We're booking 
[2-3 weeks / a few weeks] out right now — 
if you want to get on the schedule before 
the busy stretch, I can hold a spot with 
just a signed quote (no deposit needed yet). 
Interested?
```

*Note: Only use the deposit language here if that's genuinely your policy. False urgency destroys trust.*

---

**DAY 14 — Email**

**Subject line:** A neighbor of yours just finished their fence

```
Hi [First Name],

Wanted to share something since you're still 
considering your project.

[IF YOU HAVE A RECENT JOB IN THEIR AREA:]
We just finished a [6ft wood privacy fence / 
chain link / vinyl] on [nearby street or 
neighborhood] last week. If you want, I can 
send you a couple photos — same style as what 
we quoted you. Sometimes seeing it installed 
locally makes the decision easier.

[IF YOU DON'T HAVE A NEARBY JOB:]
We just wrapped up a [fence type] project 
that's very similar to what we quoted you. 
Here are a few photos from that install:
[2-3 photos attached or linked]

Also — is there anything specific holding 
you back? Sometimes I can solve a problem 
that's blocking the decision if you just 
tell me what it is.

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]
```

---

**DAY 30 — Phone Call + Voicemail Script**

*(For opened-quote leads, a call is warranted — they showed real interest)*

**Voicemail:**
```
"Hey [First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Company]. 
I'm just wrapping up your quote file — it's been about 
a month since I sent it over. I don't want to keep 
bugging you, but before I close it out I wanted to 
make sure I didn't miss anything on my end that 
kept this from moving forward. Give me a call back 
at [number] if you want to talk through it. 
Either way, no worries — take care."
```

*Followed immediately by text:*
```
Just left you a voicemail, [First Name]. 
No rush — but if the fence project is still 
on your radar at all, I'm here.
```

---

### HOT LEAD ESCALATION PROTOCOL
*(Triggered when: Lead revisits the quote link after Day 3, OR opens quote 3+ times)*

---

**Immediate Alert to You (within 5 minutes)**

GoHighLevel sends you a push notification + email:

```
🔥 HOT LEAD ALERT

[First Name Last Name] just revisited their quote.
Quote amount: $[X]
Job type: [Fence type]
Original quote date: [Date]
Times viewed: [X]
Their phone: [Number]

Call them NOW. They're actively reconsidering.
```

**Your Script for This Call:**

```
"Hey [First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Company]. 
I know it's been [X days] since I sent that quote — 
I was actually just thinking about your project. 
How are you doing? ... 

[Let them talk]

Are you getting closer to making a decision on the 
fence, or is the timing still not quite right? 

[Listen — don't pitch. Ask what's in the way.]"
```

*The goal of this call is NOT to close. It's to find out what objection exists and solve it. The fact that they revisited the quote means they want to do it — something is in the way. Find that thing.*

---

### SEQUENCE C: Seasonal Re-Engagement
*(Triggered for all LATER-tagged leads and any lead older than 30 days with no close/no response)*

**These go out 3x per year: Late February, Early June, Early September**

---

**LATE FEBRUARY — "Spring is coming" Email**

**Subject line:** Fence season starts in [X weeks] — are you on the list?

```
Hi [First Name],

Quick heads up: we start booking spring fence 
installations in [March/April], and the schedule 
fills up faster than you'd think.

You requested a quote from us [X months ago] for 
[fence type]. If you're still planning to do the 
project this year, now is actually the best time 
to lock in your spot — before the spring rush 
hits and we're booking 4-6 weeks out.

I can re-price your quote based on current 
material costs (spring pricing is usually 
favorable compared to last summer).

Want me to send an updated estimate?

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]

P.S. — If you already got the fence done, 
reply and let me know! I'll stop sending 
these and I'd love to hear how it turned out.
```

---

**EARLY JUNE — "Summer project" Text**

```
Hey [First Name] — [Your Name] from [Company]. 
Summer's here — still thinking about that fence? 
We have a few spots open in late [June/July]. 
Just reply if you want an updated quote. 
No obligation.
```

---

**EARLY SEPTEMBER — "Beat the fall rush" Email**

**Subject line:** Last good window before ground freezes

```
Hi [First Name],

Fall is actually one of the best times to 
install a fence — the ground is still workable, 
installers aren't rushed, and you'll have it 
done before winter.

[In cold climates: Once the ground freezes, 
we can't set posts until spring.]

You reached out to us earlier this year about 
[fence type]. If that project is still on your 
list, September and early October are your window.

Want me to pull up your original quote and 
update the pricing?

[Your Name]
[Company]
[Phone]
```

---

## Implementation Timeline

### Week 1: Setup
- [ ] Sign up for GoHighLevel (14-day free trial available)
- [ ] Import all current cold leads into the pipeline
- [ ] Set up quote delivery as a trackable link (not PDF attachment)
- [ ] Build Sequence A and B as email/SMS workflows in GoHighLevel
- [ ] Configure hot lead push notification to your phone

### Week 2: Customize
- [ ] Write 3 versions of the Day 3 email for your main fence types (wood, vinyl, chain link)
- [ ] Add your real photos to the Day 14 "neighbor" email
- [ ] Set up the LATER/DONE keyword triggers in SMS
- [ ] Test every automation by sending to yourself

### Week 3: Launch
- [ ] Re-enroll your existing 20 cold leads starting at the appropriate sequence point
- [ ] Run every new quote through the system from Day 0
- [ ] Schedule your three seasonal re-engagement campaigns on the calendar

### Week 4+: Optimize
- [ ] Check open rates on emails (target: 30%+ open, 10%+ reply)
- [ ] Track which message in each sequence generates the most replies
- [ ] A/B test subject lines on Day 3 and Day 14 emails

---

## Expected Outcomes

| Metric | Current | Projected (90 days) |
|--------|---------|---------------------|
| Close rate | 30% | 38-45% |
| Leads recovered per month | 0 | 3-5 |
| Additional monthly revenue | $0 | $10,500-$17,500 |
|

label: Gemini 2.5 Flash (standalone)
final_output:
As a senior strategist and analyst, I've designed a robust lead nurturing automation system tailored for your fence installation company. This system aims to significantly improve your 30% close rate by consistently engaging the 20 leads that currently go cold, leveraging smart segmentation, timely communication, and clear calls to action.

---

## Lead Nurturing Automation: Strategic Overview

**Goal:** To convert cold leads into paying customers by providing value, addressing concerns, and maintaining top-of-mind awareness through a structured, automated follow-up sequence.

**Core Principles:**
1.  **Value-Driven Engagement:** Each touchpoint offers information, builds trust, or removes friction, rather than just "checking in."
2.  **Smart Segmentation:** Messaging adapts based on lead behavior (e.g., quote opened vs. not opened).
3.  **Multi-Channel Approach:** Combining email for detailed information and text for immediate, concise communication.
4.  **Timely & Persistent:** Follows a spaced repetition model to stay relevant without being overwhelming.
5.  **Hot Signal Escalation:** Ensures immediate human intervention for highly engaged leads.
6.  **Seasonal Re-engagement:** Revives old, cold leads with relevant, time-sensitive offers.

---

## Automation Design & Flow

**Trigger:** A quote is marked as "sent" in your CRM or project management system.

**Initial Branching Logic:**
*   **Condition:** Has the lead opened the quote email or viewed the quote link?
    *   **Path A:** Quote Opened (Indicates initial interest, focus on answering questions, reinforcing value).
    *   **Path B:** Quote Not Opened (Focus on getting them to view the quote first, then provide value).

**Sequence Timeline (Both Paths):**
*   Day 1: Initial follow-up
*   Day 3: Value proposition/FAQ
*   Day 7: Social proof/addressing objections
*   Day 14: Soft urgency/incentive (if applicable)
*   Day 30: Final check-in/re-engagement offer

**Channel Mix:** Alternating between email and text messages for optimal engagement and reach.

---

## Detailed Message Sequences

Below are the specific messages designed for each path and touchpoint. Remember to replace bracketed `[placeholders]` with your actual company information and personalize where possible.

### Path A: Quote Opened

*   **Assumption:** The lead has actively engaged with your quote. Your messages should build on this interest, address potential questions, and reinforce your company's value.

**Day 1: Email - "Just Checking In on Your Fence Quote"**
*   **Subject:** Following Up: Your Fence Quote from [Your Company Name]
*   **Body:**
    Hi [Client Name],

    Hope you had a chance to look over the fence quote we sent you yesterday for your property at [Client Address/Project Type, e.g., "your backyard privacy fence"].

    We want to make sure you have all the information you need to make the best decision. If you have any initial questions about the materials, installation process, or timeline, please don't hesitate to reply to this email or give us a call at [Your Phone Number].

    You can easily access your quote again here: [Link to Quote]

    We're here to help bring your vision to life!

    Best regards,

    The Team at [Your Company Name]
    [Your Website]

**Day 3: Text - "Quick Question"**
*   **Message:** Hi [Client Name], this is [Your Name] from [Your Company Name]. Just a quick text to see if you had any initial thoughts or questions after reviewing your fence quote. Reply here if it's easier!

**Day 7: Email - "Beyond the Quote: Why Choose [Your Company Name]?"**
*   **Subject:** What Makes a [Your Company Name] Fence Different?
*   **Body:**
    Hi [Client Name],

    As you consider your options for a new fence, we wanted to share a bit more about what sets [Your Company Name] apart and ensures you get a fence that lasts.

    *   **Quality Materials:** We source only the highest-grade [mention specific materials, e.g., "Western Red Cedar," "heavy-gauge aluminum"] designed to withstand [local weather conditions].
    *   **Expert Installation:** Our certified installers are meticulous, ensuring precise alignment, strong foundations, and a clean finish every time.
    *   **Peace of Mind:** All our installations come with a [X-year] workmanship warranty, so you can rest easy knowing your investment is protected.
    *   **[Optional: Add a local benefit, e.g., "Local Expertise: We understand [local zoning laws/soil conditions] specific to [Your City/Region]."]**

    Don't just take our word for it! See what our satisfied customers are saying and browse our gallery:
    [Link to Testimonials/Reviews Page]
    [Link to Project Gallery]

    Ready to discuss specifics or schedule a site visit?
    Call us at [Your Phone Number] or reply to this email.

    Sincerely,

    The Team at [Your Company Name]
    [Your Website]

**Day 14: Text - "Last Chance for [Optional Incentive]"**
*   **Message:** Hi [Client Name], [Your Company Name] here. Reminder: If you're considering moving forward with your fence project soon, we have limited spots available for [mention a specific timeframe, e.g., "early spring installations"]. Reply "YES" if you'd like to chat about securing your spot!

**Day 30: Email - "Still Thinking About Your Fence?"**
*   **Subject:** A Month Later: Your Fence Project with [Your Company Name]
*   **Body:**
    Hi [Client Name],

    It's been about a month since we sent over your fence quote. We understand that making a significant investment like a new fence takes time and careful consideration.

    If your plans have changed, or if you've simply been too busy to decide, we're still here to help. Perhaps you're looking for a different style, material, or have a new budget in mind?

    We'd love to offer a complimentary follow-up consultation to revisit your needs, answer any lingering questions, and explore options that perfectly fit your vision and budget.

    Schedule your free consultation here: [Link to Calendar/Scheduling Page]
    Or simply reply to this email to let us know how we can assist you.

    We look forward to hearing from you!

    Best regards,

    The Team at [Your Company Name]
    [Your Website]

---

### Path B: Quote Not Opened

*   **Assumption:** The lead might have missed the email, or it went to spam. Your primary goal is to get them to view the quote, then gently guide them towards engagement.

**Day 1: Email - "Your Fence Quote is Ready!"**
*   **Subject:** Your Fence Quote from [Your Company Name] - Ready for Review!
*   **Body:**
    Hi [Client Name],

    We've just sent over your personalized quote for your fence project at [Client Address/Project Type].

    You can view all the details, including materials, scope of work, and pricing, by clicking the link below:
    [Direct Link to Quote]

    We're excited about the possibility of working with you to enhance your property. If you have any trouble accessing the quote or have immediate questions, please reply to this email or call us directly at [Your Phone Number].

    Looking forward to hearing from you!

    Sincerely,

    The Team at [Your Company Name]
    [Your Website]

**Day 3: Text - "Did You Get My Email?"**
*   **Message:** Hi [Client Name], this is [Your Name] from [Your Company Name]. Just checking if you received the fence quote I sent a couple of days ago? Sometimes emails go astray! You can view it here: [Direct Link to Quote]

**Day 7: Email - "Why Quality Fencing Matters"**
*   **Subject:** Thinking About Your New Fence? Here's What to Consider.
*   **Body:**
    Hi [Client Name],

    We still haven't seen that you've opened your fence quote, and we want to make sure you have all the resources you need. Beyond the price, choosing the right fence involves several key considerations:

    *   **Durability:** A quality fence is an investment. We use materials and techniques designed for longevity, reducing maintenance and replacement costs.
    *   **Curb Appeal:** A well-designed and installed fence significantly enhances your home's aesthetic and value.
    *   **Privacy & Security:** Get the peace of mind you deserve with a fence that truly meets your needs.

    We're confident our quote offers excellent value for a superior product and service.
    You can easily review your personalized quote here: [Direct Link to Quote]

    If you have any questions or would prefer us to walk you through it over the phone, please reply to this email or call [Your Phone Number].

    Best regards,

    The Team at [Your Company Name]
    [Your Website]

**Day 14: Text - "Quick Thought on Your Fence Project"**
*   **Message:** Hi [Client Name], [Your Company Name]
